items: What are you thankful for and what has been challenging for you since we last met?
Have you experienced God at work in your life lately?
Have you had the opportunity to care for your friends or neighbours this week?
How did you go with your application from the last time we met?
Read the passage aloud and then watch the video clip.
Have one person retell the passage in their own words with the group’s help.
Was there anything missed or added?

data.html: 1That same day Jesus went out of the house and sat by the lake.2Such large crowds gathered around him that he got into a boat and sat in it, while all the people stood on the shore.3Then he told them many things in parables, saying: “A farmer went out to sow his seed.4As he was scattering the seed, some fell along the path, and the birds came and ate it up.5Some fell on rocky places, where it did not have much soil. It sprang up quickly, because the soil was shallow.6But when the sun came up, the plants were scorched, and they withered because they had no root.7Other seed fell among thorns, which grew up and choked the plants.8Still other seed fell on good soil, where it produced a crop—a hundred, sixty or thirty times what was sown.9Whoever has ears, let them hear.”

items: What stands out for you in this passage?
What does this passage tell you about Jesus, God, people and you?
What does this passage say about responding to God?
If this passage is from God, what difference would it make in your life?
How are you going to apply this passage this week?
Who will you share this passage with?
